review_summary: The text provides enough explicit guidance for two preprocessing and augmentation questions, so no clip cut is needed.

question_bank.question: What is the standard preprocessing step for each RGB channel before feeding images to the model?
choices: Subtract the channel mean and divide by the channel standard deviation
Subtract the channel standard deviation and divide by the channel mean
Multiply by the channel mean and add the channel standard deviation
Only rescale the image height and width without changing pixel values
question_bank.explanation: Channel-wise normalization centers and rescales each input channel so the model receives values with more stable statistics. The mean is removed first, then the result is divided by the standard deviation.

question_bank.question: Why does data augmentation often improve generalization in image models?
choices: It adds label-preserving randomness, increasing effective dataset diversity
It guarantees that training loss is always lower on every epoch
It removes the need for input normalization
It converts every dataset into ImageNet automatically
question_bank.explanation: Useful augmentations keep the example recognizable while presenting varied versions of it during training. That makes memorization harder and exposes the model to a broader set of inputs for the same label.

transcript_quotes: increase the size of your data set
